steps.title: Identify the structure on each side of the blank
steps.content: The sentence before the blank is an independent clause: "To collect such data by means of social media does raise a serious ethical ______." What follows — "though terms of service stipulate … users are not informed" — is an elaboration that contrasts what platforms allow with what users know. A colon after "concern" signals that elaboration; "though" then introduces the concessive clause.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — concern, though;: places a comma before "though" and a semicolon after it, splitting the clause incorrectly and leaving no punctuation to introduce the elaboration after "concern." ✗
  • Choice B — concern, though:: the comma before "though" is too weak to introduce the elaboration, and the colon lands after "though" rather than after "concern." ✗
  • Choice C — concern, though: no colon to signal the elaborative relationship; the sentence runs on without the needed boundary. ✗
  • Choice D — concern: though: a colon correctly follows the independent clause and introduces the elaboration; "though" then opens the concessive clause. ✓
D is correct.
